task: I need to answer a short note about a delivery mix-up, and I want to make sure I cover the main point without leaving anything important out. Can you help me put together a reply that’s clear and usable?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a plain reply you can use.

## Short reply
**Subject:** Delivery mix-up

Hello,

I’m writing about the delivery issue with **[order number]**. The item I received was **[wrong / damaged / missing / not what I ordered]**.

Please let me know the next step to fix this. I would like **[replacement / refund / correct item sent]**.

Thank you,  
**[Your Name]**

## Slightly firmer version
Hello,

I’m contacting you about **[order number]**. The delivery was incorrect: **[brief description]**.

Please correct this and confirm the resolution in writing.

Thank you,  
**[Your Name]**

## What to include
- order number
- what went wrong
- what you want done
- any deadline if needed

If you want, I can make it **shorter** or **more firm**.

user_simulator.text: That’s a little bare-bones. It says the obvious, but it doesn’t really cover the practical bits I’d want in there — what exactly was wrong, whether I already tried to deal with it, and that I want it confirmed in writing. I’d rather have one clean version I can use without fiddling with it.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s one clean version you can use as-is.

---

**Subject:** Delivery mix-up — [order number]

Hello,

I’m writing about **[order number]**. The delivery was incorrect: **[brief description of what was wrong]**.

I have **[already contacted the store / not yet contacted anyone]** about this. Please let me know the next step to resolve it. I would like **[replacement / refund / correct item sent]**.

Please confirm the resolution **in writing**.

Thank you,  
**[Your Name]**

---

If you want it even tighter, you can use this version:

---

**Subject:** Delivery mix-up — [order number]

Hello,

The delivery for **[order number]** was incorrect: **[brief description]**. I would like **[replacement / refund / correct item]**.

Please confirm the resolution **in writing**.

Thank you,  
**[Your Name]**

---

If you want, I can also make it into:
- a **firmer version**
- a **very short text/email version**
- or a version for **damaged, missing, or wrong-item delivery**

user_simulator.text:
Better, but still a little too generic. I wanted one version, not a couple templates. And that bracketed stuff is exactly the part I was trying to avoid fiddling with.

Use the practical bits in the actual wording — like whether I already called or emailed, and that I want a written confirmation — but keep it as one clean note I can paste in and fill a few blanks. No extra options.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s one clean version you can paste and fill in:

---

**Subject:** Delivery mix-up — [order number]

Hello,

I’m writing about **[order number]**. The delivery was incorrect: **[what was wrong]**. I have **[already contacted / not yet contacted]** you about this.

Please correct this and confirm the resolution **in writing**. I would like **[replacement / refund / correct item sent]**.

Thank you,  
**[Your Name]**

---

If you want, I can also make it **slightly firmer** without making it longer.

user_simulator.text: Still too many blanks sitting there. I wanted the practical bits worked into the note, not just parked in brackets. Something like: I already called or emailed, I want it fixed, and I want written confirmation — all in one plain note. No second version, no extra offer. Just the usable one.

turns.agent_response:
**Subject:** Delivery mix-up — [order number]

Hello,

I’m writing about **[order number]**. The delivery was incorrect: **[what was wrong]**. I already contacted you about this and want it corrected. Please confirm the resolution **in writing**. I would like **[replacement / refund / correct item sent]**.

Thank you,  
**[Your Name]**

user_simulator.text: That’s it. Plain and usable. The only thing I’d change is maybe swap “want it corrected” for “please correct it,” but it’s close enough I can use it.
